# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

module ActionDispatch
  module Session
    class MarshalCookieStore < CookieStore
      private

      def unpacked_cookie_data(req)
        data = req.cookies[@key]
        return {} unless data

        begin
          Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(data))
        rescue
          {}
        end
      end

      def write_session(req, sid, session_data, options)
        serialized = Base64.strict_encode64(Marshal.dump(session_data))
        cookie = {}
        cookie[:value] = serialized
        cookie[:path] = options[:path] || "/"
        cookie[:domain] = options[:domain] if options[:domain]
        cookie[:expires] = options[:expire_after].from_now if options[:expire_after]
        cookie[:secure] = options[:secure] if options.key?(:secure)
        cookie[:httponly] = options[:httponly] if options.key?(:httponly)
        cookie[:same_site] = options[:same_site] if options.key?(:same_site)

        req.cookie_jar[@key] = cookie
        sid ||= generate_sid
        [sid, session_data]
      end

      def delete_session(req, session_id, options)
        req.cookie_jar.delete(@key, options)
        generate_sid unless options[:drop]
      end
    end
  end
end

Rails.application.config.session_store(
  ActionDispatch::Session::MarshalCookieStore,
  key: "_session"
)
